# DM1 external command/input references

These links are external/community references for command, input, leveling, and route hypotheses only. They are not parity proof and must not be used as authoritative evidence without verification against original DM assets, ReDMCSB source, and/or runtime debugger traces.

## Scribd: Dungeon Master Leveling Commands

- URL: https://www.scribd.com/document/830531720/Dungeon-Master-Commands
- Status: received from Daniel and metadata-fetched on 2026-05-06.
- Fetched page title/metadata: Dungeon Master Leveling Commands | PDF | Function (Mathematics) | Integer (Computer Science).
- Access/content note: Scribd rendered mostly script/HTML in lightweight fetch; do not assume full PDF content was retrieved.
- Use: possible command/leveling/input hypothesis source only.

## Verification rule

Do not claim command parity, input parity, leveling parity, or runtime breakpoint evidence from this reference alone. Any command sequence derived from it must be rechecked against ReDMCSB source (COMMAND.C, CLIKMENU.C, MOVESENS.C, champion skill/experience code) and original runtime debugger traces.

## Image/OCR follow-up: likely not FTL Dungeon Master

Daniel supplied an image from the Scribd document on 2026-05-06. OCR of page 2/9 showed commands such as dm_getmodulevarobject, dm_getmodulevarstring, dm_gettime, dm_getvarfloat, dm_getvarint, dm_getvarobject, dm_getvarstring, and dm_getvarvector.

These look like Neverwinter Nights-style Dungeon Master / module console commands, not FTL Dungeon Master (1987) input, spell, movement, or runtime evidence. Treat the Scribd document as likely irrelevant for Firestaff DM1 parity unless later pages explicitly show FTL Dungeon Master data.

## Second image/OCR follow-up: still NWN, not FTL Dungeon Master

Daniel supplied another image from the same Scribd document on 2026-05-06. OCR of page 3/9 showed commands such as dm_giveXP, dm_givegold, dm_givelevel, dm_god, dm_heal, dm_jumptopoint, dm_levelup, and dm_modifyCHA.

This further confirms the document is about Neverwinter Nights/BioWare-style DM console commands, not FTL Dungeon Master (1987). It must not be used for Firestaff DM1 input, spell, movement, leveling, or runtime parity evidence.

## Third image/OCR follow-up: D&D/NWN stat modification commands

Daniel supplied a third image from the same Scribd document on 2026-05-06. OCR showed commands such as dm_modifyCON, dm_modifyDEX, dm_modifyINT, dm_modifySTR, dm_modifyWIS, dm_modifyage, dm_modifyattackbase, and dm_modifysavefortitude.

These are D&D/Aurora/Neverwinter Nights-style DM console commands using character attributes, base attack bonus, and fortitude saves. This definitively excludes the document from Firestaff/FTL Dungeon Master parity evidence. Keep it only as a rejected external reference.

## Fourth image/OCR follow-up: NWN set-stat/faction commands

Daniel supplied another image from the same Scribd document on 2026-05-06. OCR showed commands such as dm_setINT, dm_setSTR, dm_setWIS, dm_setCR, dm_setage, dm_setattackbase, dm_setfaction, dm_setfactiondefender, and dm_setfactionenemy.

These are Neverwinter Nights / D&D Dungeon Master console commands for stats, Challenge Rating, base attack bonus, and faction targeting. They are unrelated to FTL Dungeon Master and must remain excluded from Firestaff DM1 parity evidence.

## Fifth image/OCR follow-up: NWN module/time/spell-resistance commands

Daniel supplied another image from the same Scribd document on 2026-05-06. OCR showed commands such as dm_setfactionreputation, dm_setmodulevarfloat, dm_setmodulevarint, dm_setmodulevarobject, dm_setmodulevarstring, dm_setmodulevarvector, dm_setspellresistance, and dm_settime.

These are Neverwinter Nights / Aurora-style Dungeon Master console commands for factions, module variables, spell resistance, and game time. They are unrelated to FTL Dungeon Master and remain excluded from Firestaff DM1 parity evidence.

## Dungeon Master Forum: Key Configuration / RTC key mapping

- URL: https://www.dungeon-master.com/forum/viewtopic.php?t=20299
- Status: fetched on 2026-05-06; page title: Key Configuration - Dungeon Master Forum.
- Extracted topic summary: community discussion of user key overrides through config.txt, including RTC movement/spell key names such as KEY_STEP_FORWARD, KEY_STEP_BACKWARD, KEY_TURN_LEFT, KEY_TURN_RIGHT, KEY_STEP_LEFT, KEY_STEP_RIGHT, KEY_VIEW_CYCLE, KEY_VIEW_CHARn, KEY_RUNE_CHARn, KEY_RUNE_n, KEY_RUNE_DELETE, and KEY_RUNE_CAST.
- Scope note: appears to describe RTC / Return To Chaos key configuration, not original FTL DM1 DOS/ST/Amiga runtime input handling.
- Use: acceptable as an external/community key-layout comparison or route-hypothesis reference only.
- Exclusion: do not use as original DM1 V1 input parity evidence without ReDMCSB source and original runtime confirmation.
